headline: Your supermarket beef travelled 2,400km. Ours never left NSW.
subhead: Same week from the farm. Collected fresh from your nearest hub. That's what a shorter supply chain tastes like.
hero_copy:
The big chains move beef through cold storage for 14-21 days. It travels an average of 2,400km before it reaches the shelf. By the time you buy it, nobody can tell you which farm it came from.

FarmThru beef is 100% grass-fed, from regenerative farms in NSW. It goes from paddock to pick-up in 2-3 days. You collect it from your nearest hub. And you can trace every cut back to the farm that raised it.

sections.heading: Two supply chains, side by side
sections.body:
The supermarket way: farm to feedlot to abattoir to wholesaler to distribution centre to shelf. 14-21 days. 2,400km. The farmer earns a fraction of the price.

The FarmThru way: farm to hub to you. 2-3 days. No feedlots, no warehouses, no unnecessary middlemen. The farmer earns a fairer price. You get food that's genuinely fresh.

sections.heading: What you're actually eating
sections.body:
100% grass-fed beef from Rachel's Farm in Kempsey. Heritage Berkshire pork from Bundarra Berkshires. Wild-caught salmon from Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast. Pasture-raised chicken. Eggs from Farmer Brown.

No hormones. No antibiotics. No feedlot finishing. Animals raised on open pasture, on regenerative land. Every product from a farm you can visit.

sections.heading: 2,000+ families made the switch
sections.body:
"My kids can taste the difference. And I love knowing the farmers are getting a fair price." - James, Northern Beaches

"Finally, a grocery delivery that actually cares about where the food comes from. The quality is incredible." - Sarah, Sydney

FarmThru is an online grocery store with a hub-and-collect model. Order what you want, when you want. No subscription. No lock-in.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.

creative_brief: Hero: split-screen visual. Left side: plastic-wrapped supermarket meat under fluorescent lights. Right side: fresh grass-fed steak on timber board, natural light. Below-fold: animated or illustrated supply chain comparison. Farm partner portraits. Hub pick-up lifestyle shot. Let the contrast do the selling.
